# switchboard-ai-sdk

> TypeScript SDK for connecting local applications to AI tools — Codex, Claude Code, OpenCode, and Ollama — through one API.

## Summary

switchboard-ai-sdk helps Node.js and Electron apps discover and connect to local AI tools such as Codex, Claude Code, Ollama, and OpenCode through a single, consistent interface. It normalizes discovery, auth, model selection, chat requests, and health checks across providers so apps don't need provider-specific glue code.

Primary use cases:

- local LLM integration for Electron and desktop apps
- unified TypeScript API for multiple local AI providers
- agent-tool discovery on developer machines
- local HTTP bridge for AI tooling
- replacing paid hosted APIs with locally installed AI tools

## Primary Docs

- Docs site: https://mauriceheinze.github.io/switchboard-ai-sdk/
- Repository: https://github.com/MauriceHeinze/switchboard-ai-sdk
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/switchboard-ai-sdk
- Getting started: https://mauriceheinze.github.io/switchboard-ai-sdk/guide/getting-started
- API reference: https://mauriceheinze.github.io/switchboard-ai-sdk/api/reference
- Full docs (llms-full.txt): https://mauriceheinze.github.io/switchboard-ai-sdk/llms-full.txt

## Key Terms

- local AI SDK
- TypeScript AI SDK
- Electron AI integration
- Ollama integration
- Codex integration
- Claude Code integration
- OpenCode integration
- local LLM discovery
- desktop app AI
- local-first AI

## Public API

- `configure(config)`: set provider-level defaults
- `discover()`: list available providers, models, and capabilities
- `connect(providerId)`: connect to a specific AI tool
- Connected tool methods: `chat()`, `health()`, `checkAuth()`, `startAuth()`
- `startSwitchboardServer(options)`: expose the API over a local HTTP server
- `createSwitchboardServer(options)`: create a server without starting it

## Supported Providers

- Codex (agent)
- Claude Code (agent)
- Ollama (runtime)
- OpenCode (agent)
